title: The point of knee plylons
wikitext:
[]: The point of knee plylons             Quote Post by UltraBlackPilot » Fri Nov 20, 2009 7:40 pm I was looking at a pic of Unit 01, an it just occurred to me, why do Evangelions need knee pylons(if thats what there called)? They don't seem to do anything, are they just there to look cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Fri Nov 20, 2009 8:07 pm sorry for OT but i just looked at your Avatar and saw the answer you were looking for :/ nah, srsly i don`t got a clue

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Fri Nov 20, 2009 8:32 pm I imagine it makes it a million times easier for the pilot to kneel down. Also, doing a driving knee kick makes it that much more painful with knee pylons.

[]:             Quote Post by UltraBlackPilot » Fri Nov 20, 2009 8:34 pm Ironfoot wrote: Also, doing a driving knee kick makes it that much more painful with knee pylons.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Fri Nov 20, 2009 8:46 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: But they never treid that before? (I think)

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Sat Nov 21, 2009 4:46 am Protection? They're also used as weapons in both the manga and Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Nov 21, 2009 11:12 am Where it gets really weird is when you notice that they move independently of the shins. Why would that be useful as a weapon or as some sort of armor/bracing?

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Sat Nov 21, 2009 1:07 pm Just easier on the knees for crouching down in a firing position I guess. Or it could just be a visual thing, many mecha (i.e. most gundam mobile suits) have seemingly useless armor attachments.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sat Nov 21, 2009 2:45 pm I always thought it was intended so an Angel wouldn't get smart and use a cutting weapon to hack an Eva's legs off at the knees...

[]:             Quote Post by Jesterhead » Sat Nov 21, 2009 3:17 pm CyberXIII wrote: I always thought it was intended so an Angel wouldn't get smart and use a cutting weapon to hack an Eva's legs off at the knees...

[]:             Quote Post by Orphan Of Darkness » Sun Nov 22, 2009 4:48 am Given the added weight of the pylons and upper body armor I believe they would be necessary for crouching and balance in other various positioning; as previously mentioned. They do provide an important joint with extra armor and shock insulation as well I guess. Also, they look neat.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Nov 22, 2009 2:04 pm They just see to be stylised kneepads to me. Or extended shin guards.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Nov 22, 2009 3:28 pm CyberXIII wrote: I always thought it was intended so an Angel wouldn't get smart and use a cutting weapon to hack an Eva's legs off at the knees...

[]:             Quote Post by UltraBlackPilot » Sun Nov 22, 2009 10:02 pm CyberXIII wrote: I always thought it was intended so an Angel wouldn't get smart and use a cutting weapon to hack an Eva's legs off at the knees...

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sun Nov 22, 2009 11:04 pm 1. Stops the knees bending the wrong way. 2. Added "knee to the face" damage. 3. Looks good. 4. lol plylons.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:52 am UltraBlackPilot wrote: Ironfoot wrote: Also, doing a driving knee kick makes it that much more painful with knee pylons.

[]:             Quote Post by Rock-Slash » Mon Nov 23, 2009 3:01 am Im going for the obvious answer and say is just to look cool, those pylons make the Eva even more heavy and give less movility, it also doesn't offer any kind of protection.

[]:             Quote Post by jayngfet » Wed Nov 25, 2009 10:13 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: Ironfoot wrote: Also, doing a driving knee kick makes it that much more painful with knee pylons.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Tue Dec 01, 2009 5:32 pm Because an engineer was walking past Gendo's office one day, and could have sworn they heard "You must construct additional pylons"

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Dec 01, 2009 8:01 pm Teague wrote: "You must construct additional pylons"
